cards.front: What is the chemical formula for water?
cards.back:
D. H2O

Water is H2O — two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

cards.front: What does DNA stand for?
cards.back:
A. Deoxyribonucleic Acid

DNA = Deoxyribonucleic Acid, the molecule of heredity.

cards.front: Which organelle is the powerhouse of the cell?
cards.back:
C. Mitochondria

Mitochondria produce ATP through cellular respiration.

cards.front: Can a Programmable Logic Device implement a logic function that is unknown at the time of its manufacture?
cards.back: No, it can only implement a logic function that is known at the time of its manufacture
cards.question: Can a Programmable Logic Device implement a logic function that is unknown at the time of its manufacture?
cards.answer: No, it can only implement a logic function that is known at the time of its manufacture

cards.front: What are the two main components of a Programmable Logic Device (PLD)?
cards.back: An AND array and an OR array.
cards.question: What are the two main components of a Programmable Logic Device (PLD)?
cards.answer: An AND array and an OR array.

cards.front: What are the main components of a PLD?
cards.back: An AND array and an OR array to provide an AND-OR sum of products implementation.
cards.question: What are the main components of a PLD?
cards.answer: An AND array and an OR array to provide an AND-OR sum of products implementation.

cards.front: What are the main components of a Programmable Logic Device (PLD)?
cards.back: A PLD is an integrated circuit with programmable gates divided into an AND array and an OR array
cards.question: What are the main components of a Programmable Logic Device (PLD)?
cards.answer: A PLD is an integrated circuit with programmable gates divided into an AND array and an OR array

cards.front: What are the primary components of a PLD?
cards.back: An AND array and an OR array
cards.question: What are the primary components of a PLD?
cards.answer: An AND array and an OR array

cards.front: How does a PROM differ from a ROM?
cards.back: A PROM is programmable but read-only, whereas a ROM is read-only and permanent binary information must be specified by the designer
cards.question: How does a PROM differ from a ROM?
cards.answer: A PROM is programmable but read-only, whereas a ROM is read-only and permanent binary information must be specified by the designer

cards.front: How are Boolean functions implemented in PROMs?
cards.back: using s or ICs electrically
cards.question: How are Boolean functions implemented in PROMs?
cards.answer: using s or ICs electrically

cards.front: What is the main purpose of using Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM) in Programmable Logic Devices (PLDs)?
cards.back: To store permanent binary information that must be specified by the designer, which is then embedded in the unit to form the required interconnection pattern
cards.question: What is the main purpose of using Programmable Read Only Memory (PROM) in Programmable Logic Devices (PLDs)?
cards.answer: To store permanent binary information that must be specified by the designer, which is then embedded in the unit to form the required interconnection pattern

cards.front: What happens to the stored data in a PROM when it is powered off?
cards.back: The data is lost forever, as PROMs are read-only memory devices
cards.question: What happens to the stored data in a PROM when it is powered off?
cards.answer: The data is lost forever, as PROMs are read-only memory devices

cards.front: How are minterms generated in a PROM, given that the inputs of AND gates are fixed and non-programmable?
cards.back: Minterms are generated by using an nx2n decoder to implement 2n product terms
cards.question: How are minterms generated in a PROM, given that the inputs of AND gates are fixed and non-programmable?
cards.answer: Minterms are generated by using an nx2n decoder to implement 2n product terms

cards.front: What are the units of the fundamental or base quantities called?
cards.back: fundamental or base units
cards.question: What are the units of the fundamental or base quantities called?
cards.answer: fundamental or base units

cards.front: What is the primary purpose of using units in measurement?
cards.back: The primary purpose of using units in measurement is to compare a physical quantity with a certain basic, arbitrarily chosen, internationally accepted reference standard called a unit.
cards.question: What is the primary purpose of using units in measurement?
cards.answer: The primary purpose of using units in measurement is to compare a physical quantity with a certain basic, arbitrarily chosen, internationally accepted reference standard called a unit.

cards.front: Why are only a limited number of units needed to express all physical quantities?
cards.back: All physical quantities are inter-related with one another, so only a limited number of base units are needed.
cards.question: Why are only a limited number of units needed to express all physical quantities?
cards.answer: All physical quantities are inter-related with one another, so only a limited number of base units are needed.

cards.front: What are the three fundamental units of the International System of Units (SI)?
cards.back: metre, kilogram and second
cards.question: What are the three fundamental units of the International System of Units (SI)?
cards.answer: metre, kilogram and second
